codeReview.summary: The refactor fixes the shared MCP caller-role problem and explicitly tags service-generated CreateWork paths, but it still misses the required live StreamableHTTP/session.CallTool regression proof and regresses serve-mode broadcast propagation for newly created sessions.

issues.description: The new "integration" test never drives the live transport. Its body only builds requests and inspects GetServerForRequest/GetServerInstance; there is no handler.ServeHTTP or session.CallTool invocation, so the requested end-to-end proof for external and supervisor-marked traffic is still missing.

SessionManager.SetBroadcastFunc only updates servers that already exist. serve.go installs it before any sessions are materialized (internal/cli/serve.go:283-296), so later-created sessions such as the "channel-send" server never inherit the hub broadcast function and SendChannelEvent falls back to stdout instead of reaching serve-mode websocket clients.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/library/contracts.md still records the MCP supervisor provenance gap, but it does not capture the now-required explicit CreatedBy="service" rule for true service-generated follow-on work. That makes the service-provenance fix easier to miss in later reviews.

The control-plane-worker procedure calls for serve-path validation and agent-browser checks for features that touch serve, but the worker transcript/handoff shows only unit tests plus make lint/build; interactiveChecks is empty and there is no recorded ./build/fase serve --port 5380 smoke test.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl skeleton for ce529bb0-458b-43a6-9d62-18347ab468e9; handoff verification block; .factory/services.yaml: serve.start/healthcheck

summary: FAIL: the fix improves MCP session isolation and service provenance, but it still lacks the required live transport regression test and breaks broadcast propagation for newly created serve-mode sessions.
